reference: GATE CS — Computer Networks / Network Layer

formulas.name: ARP (Address Resolution)
formulas.plain: ARP maps a known IP to its MAC within the same LAN: broadcast request, unicast reply, cached
formulas.whenToUse: Delivering a frame on the local link when only the IP is known.
formulas.trap: ARP works only within one broadcast domain; for remote IPs you ARP for the default gateway's MAC.

formulas.name: DHCP (DORA Handshake)
formulas.plain: DHCP assigns IP/mask/gateway/DNS via Discover, Offer, Request, Ack; UDP ports 67 (server) / 68 (client)
formulas.whenToUse: Dynamic host configuration and lease questions.
formulas.trap: Discover and Request are broadcast (client has no IP yet); uses UDP, not TCP.

formulas.name: ICMP (Diagnostics)
formulas.plain: ICMP reports IP-layer errors and supports ping (echo) and traceroute (TTL-limited probes)
formulas.trap: ICMP is carried inside IP but is a network-layer protocol; it does not make IP reliable.

formulas.name: NAT / PAT
formulas.plain: NAT rewrites private<->public IPs; PAT (overloading) multiplexes many hosts onto one public IP via ports
formulas.whenToUse: Reasoning about private addressing and address conservation.
formulas.trap: NAT breaks end-to-end addressing and complicates inbound connections; it is not a security feature per se.
